hero.label: Visual Bookmark Manager
hero.headline: Turn links into a visual board.
hero.description: Save videos, social posts, articles, and images in one click, then arrange them freely. Turn a plain list into an inspiration board you grasp at a glance. Free, no sign-up.

problem.headline: You pile them up and never look again.
problem.body: Just links. Feeds you scroll right past. Don't let what you save get buried.

capture.title: To your board, from anywhere.
capture.body: Just paste a URL — X, YouTube, articles, shops, images, anything on the web. Want one-click saving? The browser extension and bookmarklet have you covered.

layout.title: Arrange it freely.
layout.body: Drag and drop to set size and position exactly how you like.

live.title: A board that comes alive.
live.body: The video in focus plays for real while the others shimmer as stills — not a list of stills, but a living visual board. One switch, MOTION, settles it back into a quiet moodboard.

organize.title: Tag to sort.
organize.body: Filter by tag to surface just what you want. More looks and themes are on the way.

privacy.title: No account. Fully local.
privacy.body: Everything lives inside your browser. No account, nothing to sign up for — your privacy stays yours.

share.headline: Share it as one board.
share.body: Export your finished board as an image, or share a link — your exact layout arrives just as you made it.

meta.title: Features
meta.description: Save anything from the web and lay it out as a visual board. Inline playback, tags, instant sharing — all private, all in your browser.

hero.title: Everything you save, made to look at.
hero.lead: AllMarks keeps the things you love from across the web on one living, visual board — not a list you never open again.

save.heading: Save three ways
save.body: Three ways to save: paste a URL onto your board, use the bookmarklet, or use the Chrome extension. With the bookmarklet or the extension, one click saves the page and lets you tag it on the spot.

board.heading: A board you arrange
board.body: Resize any card freely, or pick from a range of layout presets. Tag freely; tags sort alphabetically, or drag them into your own order.

motion.heading: A board that comes alive
motion.body: As you browse, the video in focus plays for real while the others shimmer through still frames — so the board feels alive without ever turning into chaos. One switch, MOTION, calms it all to stillness whenever you want.

share.heading: Share in a link
share.body: Turn any board into a short link with a ready-made preview image for social. Or export it as a PNG and post it to X. Shared boards live on a link, not in an account.

privacy.heading: Private by default
privacy.body: Your bookmarks live in your browser. No account, no sign-up, no tracking cookies. Free to use, and your data stays with you.

sites.heading: Where it works
sites.body: Posts from X, YouTube (including Shorts), TikTok, Vimeo, and SoundCloud play right on your board. Anything else is saved with a clean thumbnail.
sites.note: Instagram can't be embedded from outside Instagram by anyone, so Instagram saves open the original page.

meta.title: About
meta.description: AllMarks turns your bookmarks into a visual collage. Private by design — your data stays in your browser. Free, no account.

hero.title: Bookmarks, made to be seen.
hero.lead: AllMarks is a place to keep the things you love from across the web — and actually enjoy looking at them.

philosophy.heading: Express, don't file
philosophy.body: Most bookmarks vanish into a list you never open again. AllMarks treats them as something to arrange, revisit, and share — a visual board, not a folder.

privacy.heading: Private by design
privacy.body: Your bookmarks live in your browser. No account, no sign-up. Free to use, and your data stays with you.

openSource.heading: Open source
openSource.body: AllMarks is open source. Anyone can read the code and confirm that nothing is sent anywhere it shouldn't be.

meta.title: Guide
meta.description: How to use AllMarks: save in three ways, arrange your board, play media, and share — start in about 30 seconds.

hero.title: Up and running in 30 seconds.
hero.lead: No account, no setup. Open the board, save your first link, and start arranging.

start.heading: Open the board
start.body: Head to the board and you're in — nothing to sign up for. Copy a link from anywhere on the web and paste it straight onto the board to save your first card.

save.heading: Three ways to save
save.body: There are three ways to save: paste a URL onto your board, add the bookmarklet to your bookmarks bar, or use the Chrome extension. With the bookmarklet or the extension, one click saves the page and lets you tag it on the spot.

arrange.heading: Make it yours
arrange.body: Click a card to open it full-size. Resize cards or pick a layout preset, and add tags. Drag cards and tags into the order you like — everything stays on a tidy grid.

play.heading: Watch it come alive
play.body: Videos from X, YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo, and SoundCloud play right on the board — the one in focus plays for real while the rest shimmer as stills. Toggle MOTION off any time to settle into a quiet moodboard.

share.heading: Share your board
share.body: Make a short link with a preview image for social, or export the board as a PNG to post on X. Anyone with the link sees your board — no account needed on their end either.

data.heading: About your data
data.body: Everything is stored in this browser only. That means it won't follow you to another device or a private window, and clearing your browser data clears your boards. You can export a backup any time.

trouble.heading: If something looks off
trouble.body: A card stuck on a plain thumbnail usually just needs the page reloaded so it can fetch a fresh preview. If a bookmarklet save does nothing, re-add the bookmarklet — older copies can go stale after an update.

meta.title: FAQ
meta.description: Common questions about AllMarks: is it free, where your data lives, how saving and sharing work, and which browsers are supported.

hero.title: Questions, answered.
hero.lead: The short version: it's free, private, and lives in your browser. Here are the details.

q1.a: Yes — AllMarks is free, with no account and no sign-up.

q2.a: Your board is stored only in your browser, on your device — not on a server. The one exception is when you share: a copy of the chosen cards' URL info (title, preview image, and the like) is held for about 30 days, then auto-deleted. Since there's no account, no personal information is ever included. If you don't share, nothing leaves your device.

q3.a: Three ways: paste a URL onto the board, use the bookmarklet in any browser, or use the Chrome extension. The bookmarklet and the extension save in one click.

q4.a: Any modern browser works for the board and the bookmarklet. The one-click extension is Chrome-based for now.

q5.q: Can I use it on my phone?
q5.a: We're still optimizing the mobile layout. Saving itself — pasting a URL onto the board, or using the bookmarklet — works, but the look will be polished a bit later.

q6.a: When you share, you can turn a copy of the chosen cards into a short link with a preview image for social — or export a PNG to post on X. Whoever opens the link just sees that copy; they can't reach your device or your board. No account on either side.

q7.q: What happens if I clear my browser data?
q7.a: Because everything lives in your browser, clearing its data also clears your boards. Export a backup first if you want to keep them, and import it later to restore.

q8.a: Yes. The code is open, so anyone can confirm that nothing is sent anywhere it shouldn't be.

q9.a: No. There's no account creation or login at all.

meta.title: Chrome Extension
meta.description: Save any page to AllMarks in one tap with the Chrome extension. Reads only the page you save, sends nothing to a server.

hero.title: Save anything, in one tap.
hero.lead: The AllMarks extension puts a save button on every page, so the things you find go straight to your board.

status.label: Coming soon to the Chrome Web Store
status.body: The extension is on its way to the store. In the meantime you can already save from any browser with the bookmarklet — no waiting.

what.heading: One-tap saving
what.body: Save the page you're on with a click, a right-click menu, or a keyboard shortcut. It drops straight onto your board, and you can add a tag right as you save.

how.heading: How to get it
how.body: Once it's on the Chrome Web Store, it's a one-click install. Right now, grab the bookmarklet from the guide and you'll have the same one-tap saving in any browser today.

sites.heading: Works across the web
sites.body: Save from anywhere. Posts from X, YouTube, Vimeo, SoundCloud, and more come in ready to play on your board; everything else saves with a clean preview.

privacy.heading: Reads only what you save
privacy.body: The extension reads the page you choose to save and nothing else. There's no server in the middle — your saves go straight into your browser.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: AllMarks collects zero personal data. Your bookmarks stay in your browser; nothing is uploaded unless you choose to share a board.

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.lead: AllMarks is built on one principle: your data belongs to you. We collect no personal data, and there are no accounts.

philosophy.heading: Zero data collection
philosophy.body: Your bookmarks, tags, and board layouts are stored only in your browser's local database (IndexedDB). We do not collect, store, or transmit your personal data to our servers, and there are no accounts to sign in to.

collect.heading: What we don't collect
collect.body: We do not collect your name, email, or address; we have no accounts or login credentials; we keep no copy of your browsing history or your bookmarks on our servers; we use no tracking or advertising cookies; and we gather no IP-based fingerprints or analytics tied to you as an individual.

local.heading: Local storage only
local.body: Everything lives in your browser on your device. That means we cannot access, view, or recover your data, no server-side backup of your boards exists, and clearing your browser data permanently removes your AllMarks content. You can export your own backup at any time.

sharing.heading: When you share a board
sharing.body: Sharing is the one case where data leaves your device — and only because you ask it to. When you create a share link, the board's data (just the cards you chose to share, with no personal information, since there are no accounts) is uploaded to our hosting (Cloudflare KV) along with a preview image (Cloudflare R2), so the people you send the link to can open the board. It is reachable only through the secret link and is automatically deleted after about 30 days, so nothing lingers. Nothing is uploaded unless you create a share link, and your private boards are never uploaded. You can also export a board as a PNG, generated entirely in your browser.

bookmarklet.heading: Bookmarklet
bookmarklet.body: The AllMarks bookmarklet reads the Open Graph meta tags (title, description, image, site name) of the page you're on, entirely within your browser, and passes them to your local AllMarks save window. No data passes through our servers.

extension.heading: Browser extension
extension.body: The AllMarks extension reads only the page you choose to save, and sends nothing to any server — your saves go straight into your browser's local database. Its own privacy policy explains exactly what it reads and the permissions it uses.

hosting.heading: Hosting and analytics
hosting.body: AllMarks is hosted on Cloudflare Pages. Cloudflare may record minimal, anonymized server metrics (such as total page views) as part of standard hosting; these contain no personally identifiable information. We use no Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, and no third-party tracking scripts.

thirdParty.heading: Third-party content
thirdParty.body: AllMarks can display content from other sites — for example tweets via react-tweet, or videos via oEmbed. Those embeds may be subject to the privacy policies of their own services, which we recommend reviewing for any content that appears in your boards.

advertising.heading: Advertising
advertising.body: AllMarks is free and shows no ads today. If we ever introduce advertising to support the free service, we will update this policy, clearly label it, and continue to collect zero personal data on our end.

children.heading: Children's privacy
children.body: AllMarks does not knowingly collect any information from children under 13. Because we collect no personal data from anyone, this is inherent to how the app works.

changes.heading: Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Changes are posted on this page with a new "last updated" date. Because we collect no personal data, we cannot notify you of changes directly.

contact.heading: Questions
contact.body: If you have questions about this policy, please reach us through the contact page.

meta.title: Terms of Service
meta.description: AllMarks terms of service — free to use, no account, your data stays in your browser.

hero.title: Terms of Service
hero.lead: AllMarks is a free app with no account. These terms explain the basics of using it.

acceptance.heading: 1. Acceptance of terms
acceptance.body: By using AllMarks ("the Service"), you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

service.heading: 2. The service
service.body: AllMarks is a free web app for saving bookmarks and arranging them as visual boards. All data is stored locally in your browser, and no account or registration is required.

responsibilities.heading: 3. Your responsibilities
responsibilities.body: You are responsible for the content you save and share, you agree not to use the Service for any unlawful purpose or to disrupt it, and you understand that your data is stored locally — clearing your browser data permanently deletes your AllMarks content.

ip.heading: 4. Intellectual property
ip.body: Bookmarks you save reference content owned by third parties; AllMarks claims no ownership of bookmarked content. The AllMarks app, its code, design, and branding belong to AllMarks.

sharing.heading: 5. Sharing content
sharing.body: When you create a share link, the board data is uploaded to our hosting so the link can render it, and is deleted automatically after about 30 days. By sharing, you represent that you have the right to share the content in this way.

warranty.heading: 6. No warranty
warranty.body: The Service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee it will be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free, and we are not responsible for data loss from browser data clearing, device failure, or any other cause.

liability.heading: 7. Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, AllMarks shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the Service.

modifications.heading: 8. Changes to the service and terms
modifications.body: We may modify or discontinue the Service at any time without notice, and may update these terms from time to time. Continued use after changes means you accept the revised terms.

law.heading: 9. Governing law
law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of Japan, and any disputes are subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Tokyo, Japan.

contact.heading: 10. Questions
contact.body: If you have questions about these terms, please reach us through the contact page.

meta.title: Contact
meta.description: Get in touch about AllMarks — questions, feedback, bug reports, and security, through GitHub.

hero.title: Get in touch.
hero.lead: Questions, ideas, and bug reports are all welcome. GitHub is the most reliable way to reach us.

github.label: GitHub Issues
github.body: For bug reports and feature requests, open an issue on GitHub — it's the surest way to be heard.

feedback.label: Feedback
feedback.body: Ideas for making AllMarks better are always welcome — "I wish it could do this" or "this part is awkward," however small.

security.label: Security
security.body: Found a security issue? Please report it privately through GitHub rather than a public issue. Responsible disclosure is much appreciated.

meta.title: Extension Privacy Policy
meta.description: Privacy policy for the AllMarks Chrome extension — it reads only the page you save and sends nothing to any server.

hero.title: AllMarks Extension — Privacy Policy
hero.lead: The extension reads only the page you choose to save, and sends nothing to any server.

does.heading: What the extension does
does.body: When you click the extension icon, press the keyboard shortcut, or use the right-click "Save to AllMarks" menu, the extension reads the URL, page title, description, preview image, site name, and favicon of the active tab (or the link you right-clicked) and writes them as a bookmark into your browser's local database on the allmarks.app origin.

notCollect.heading: What it does not do
notCollect.body: It does not collect, log, or transmit your browsing history. It sends no data to our servers or any third party. It has no analytics, tracking, advertising, or telemetry of any kind. It does not read page contents beyond the Open Graph meta tags above, and only when you trigger a save.

storage.heading: Where data is stored
storage.body: Saved bookmarks live only in your browser's local database on the allmarks.app origin. Your extension settings (such as the floating button and cursor pill options) are saved with chrome.storage.sync, so if Chrome sync is on they may sync across your Chrome browsers through Google's infrastructure — we never see them. A local list of which URLs you've already saved is kept with chrome.storage.local so the save button can show an "already saved" state; it stays on your device. There is no account, server, or database on our side that stores your bookmarks.

bridge.heading: How saving reaches the app
bridge.body: To write a bookmark into the same local database the AllMarks web app uses, the extension opens a hidden page at allmarks.app/save-iframe. This bridges from the extension into the allmarks.app origin; no information is sent to any other domain.

perms.heading: Permissions and why
perms.activeTab: Read the URL and Open Graph meta of the tab you actively save.
perms.contextMenus: Add the right-click "Save to AllMarks" entries.
perms.scripting: Inject the small extractor that reads the page's meta tags when you save.
perms.offscreen: Create the offscreen page that hosts the allmarks.app save bridge.
perms.storage: Save your extension settings and the local "already saved" list.
perms.hostAll: Show the floating save button on the pages you visit and let the save bridge reach allmarks.app. The extension reads a page's meta only when you choose to save it.

openSource.heading: Open source
openSource.body: The source code is public on GitHub (the extension lives under the extension folder), so anyone can verify exactly what it does.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.body: For privacy questions, please use the contact page.

changes.heading: Changes
changes.body: We update this page whenever permissions or data flows change. The "last updated" date above reflects the most recent change.
